reviews.review_msg: Chariandy was genuinely excited by the topics and expressed compassion for students. Thought-provoking books, even if I didn't love them all. He let students talk too much then sometimes end would feel rushed, but he posted the slides after. The essay prompt was harder to grasp but was on topic with the course, and he was always open to questions.

I took the course for my interest in creative writing. Professor Chariandy is a great and funny professor who is easy to speak with. I learned a lot in his class and about his background in creative writing. It was an exam free class too, which was amazing and relaxing for me. People who are interested in creative writing should take this class!

Professor Chariandy taught about Black Canadian Literature and did an excellent job. I feel as though our discussions were clear, focused and taught me a lot more than what I came into class with. He did not enable students with their entitled opinions and instead educated them in a respectful manner. Amazing Prof!!

don't really like his method of teaching. he's nice but his feedback isn't constructive AT ALL. bruh, i didn't come to get my ego boosted, i came to improve my writing. he changes his syllabus every few weeks which was annoying. no clear grading criteria. he also rarely emails back. i learnt mainly from the textbook. lectures were only discussion.

Prof Chariandy is so nice and makes everyone feel like their opinions and ideas are valued. His lectures can sometimes be boring, but it's English so you can't blame him. I suggest attending class (I did not and found it harder to study for final) as he bases 1/3 of the final on lecture notes and class discussion.

A caring and enthusiastic prof! While the criteria for projects like the creative project were vague (no rubric or anything), you will get a high mark if you participate in class and put effort into your work. He is a master at making your ideas seem profound during class discussion- you will never feel awkward for participating in lectures.
He is absolutely amazing. Very respectable, polite and knows the material he teaches well. LOVED him. One of the best no doubt, take a class with him you won't regret it!

David was my favourite professor this semester, he was not my TA unfortunately as I really enjoyed discussing the texts with him. My only regret was that it was a 100 level class as I felt the material was so amazing we would have had plenty of material to delve in deeper with. He is very encouraging and I would 100% take another of his classes.

Awesome teacher. I couldn't possibly recommend him more. Especially if you're into creative writing. He gives great advice on how to improve your writing and different things to consider in your own writing.

Best course I took as an undergrad at SFU. Chariandy is extremely insightful, and though soft spoken, most everything he says is engaging and thought provoking. He is great as both an intellectual and a teacher; he makes complex theories intelligible and creates an environment where each student can contribute to class. Gives valuable essayfeedback

His class can be somewhat boring because of the books we read, but it's an interesting course considering everyone gets to express how they feel about each book. David is always very supportive of the answers also. He will never make you feel uncomfortable or unwanted.

Has to be the most boring lectures I've ever attended at SFU. Absolutely wonderful guy though. One on one, he makes everything clear and is extremely helpful in getting you to improve your writing. Such a sweetheart and is very respectful of all of his students. I don't think I heard one negative thing come from his mouth all semester.

David has to be the most soft spoken prof I ever took a class with.  But despite his initial shy demeanor, once he gets on a roll, he is interesting, passionate, and funny.  It was a pleasure attending lectures and tutorials.  And yes, he has great eyes.

This class was a little disappointing. I'd heard great things about him, but his lectures were dry and he never got on the students who never contributed to discussion like he should have(its 3rd yr, you'd better speak up!), no real discussion was had. He was always late 10mins for both lec and tut. ALWAYS. Texts were good. Nice guy though.
Dave Chariandy is a hero. I would gladly fight beside him as a brother in arms.
Best class I've ever taken, he was an excellent prof and I would definately take more classes with him.
SO BORING. His class was a snorefest. I tried to listen to him lecture and although he has some insightful things to say, his voice just puts me to sleep. I'm sorry.

David is obviously interested in his material and what his students have to say about it. He wants you to like his classes and do well. However, he's got a lot going on in his life, and it seemed as if the class was the last thing on his mind. Marking turnaround was a month, guest speakers appeared often,and student presentations led the class.
let us celebrate ourselves, our ethnicities ... no need for much thinking, come along for the ride, smile and say positive things, and no need to read the books
good marker, interesting lectures, nicest eyes ever.

David is a very knowledgable, caring professor. He is always there for extra help and his exams are straightforward. Hard marker of essays, but all in all very interesting texts. He seems to REALLY care about his students and listen to their opinions.

Great teacher, extremely knowledgeable and well-researched, classes and readings are always interesting and he's very respectful of students' opinions. One of the best profs I've had. Take his classes if you get the chance, you won't regret it.

To say the least, David makes you identify with the material -- it's like you're bound to feel it somewhere in your past history, or present. But once you're there, you discover that post-colonial life is one big pile of complicated, diabolical #### and david is the symbol of this mess. Otherwise, he marks fairly if you parrot the secondary sources

agreed, his quizzes came out of "left field." david should've mentioned them in the course syllabus or several times in the weeks leading up to them. plus, he asked hard questions that i couldn't answer because i forgot to read the books. seriously though, this was a great class and david is a great prof.

David is a good teacher, and an interesting person. Class was never dull. He gives strange quizzes though, they came out of left field and damaged my mark. Otherwise I recommend David highly, just watch out for the quizzes.
David's great. Very smart, very helpful, and very unorthodox. He doesn't get caught up in teaching his class like everyone else, but makes it fun with presentations, films, and great conversation.

Supposed to be a seminar, but professor treated group like children in elementary school.  Good films, good books but professor far too controlling for free expression.  Not recommended for free thinkers.
Made the lectures actually interesting. Really tried to get the class to participate. Assignments and exams were fair. Books were quite boring, but his teaching made up for it.
Fantastic! Books were great. Lectures and material were so easy to follow. Challenging.

Every year, my English teachers get better and better.  This year was no different.  David is by far, the best English teacher you will ever encounter with.  Plus, u can easily mistake him for a student.  Wears nice clothes, carries backpack, always smiling
Chariandy rules!!!!  He is hot and cool and smart, great way to start off the SFU experience.

This prof knows his stuff!  I dunno if he makes it up or not, but his lechures are so interesting to hear.  The final is not hard, and the books he picks for you is quite interesting.  He doesn't make you read too much, and he encourages feedback and class discussions.  Overall, he's a great prof!
He's got a great aura. = )! Very nice. Very approachable...

Very enthusiactic ! Helpful and tries to engage people into the conversations in tutorial.  Very funni, and is able to make everything clear, simple, and straight to the point.  Isn't biased when marking, u just need to make a strong argument, and not necessarily in favour of his ideas or thoughts.

He's absolutely ADORABLE! Lectures are very well organized and he's so enthusiastic! ALways smiley and values all student opinions. Interesting choice of works and intertextual references. Incorporated a lot of neat films clips and works of art! My TA was pretty brutal though on 1st essay. BEWARE!
Excited about course content, adorable, and acts like everything you say is the most important thing he's ever heard.  A good teacher for an elementary course.

I only paid attention or went to class because we were studying two of my favourite writers - Otherwise I would have slept the whole semester -Nothing new to teach - Wait, are we in Kindergarten still? Def. felt like a bookclub -except we got to watch some excellent films!

Being in Science, i'd have to say, this was the one class i DID look forward to. David's very enthusiastic about what he teaches, which makes things all the more interesting for the class. The reading was (mostly) very good, didnt feel like homework at all. It's a pretty easy couse, well worth it!
This class was a book club.

Lectures provide some insight on different literary terms however not as insightful as i had hoped.  If you are coming from a strong eng 12 background this is an easy A.  IF you took IB english or AP english...you will SAIL through this course no problem..just watch out for MLA style guidelines

Lectures are obvious and somewhat uninteresting. Teaches like everyone just hatched out of highschool, i guess for those who did it might be of some value. T.A. was horrible and can only blame Chariandy. Chariandy was enthusiastic and very helpful of what the class requires however.

Comments bang on. hey, I don't mind the teddy bear appeal. Everything quite clear, maybe too clear. This isn't first year. Make me me think, damn it, don't do it for me. Didn't learn anything new in the class.
cute as tellytubby and about as insightful... To begin with quite liked the cheerleader stuff... doesn't really make you think, gives you ready  made answers... all rather simple
awesome prof..very clear on everything, making the class that much easier
GOOD teacher.  I was never good at english, but he makes me WANT to go to class :)
No Comments
SUCH a good teacher.  very clear on his subjects and is a very interesting person over all.  love his class to death, so much fun.  and he makes the teaching seem like a hobby, instead of a job.

David not only expresses genuine interest in his subject matter, but also in the learning experience and environment of his students. He is always available to help if you need it. Intersting, educational and fun. Excellent guest speakers and field trips.

David has a passion for his field, great sense of humour and never makes your ideas seem invalid.Lots of videos and a field trip or two. Is really there for his students. His partner Sophie McCall (Can Lit) is of equal calliber.
loves his material, and makes class fun and interesting.  Great Prof!
